page: Amazon Web Services r8id.metal-48xl is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server powered by Intel Xeon 6975P-C processors. Built on the x86_64 architecture, this instance features 192 dedicated vCPUs across 96 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. It operates at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz and includes a 480 MB L3 cache. The hardware is equipped with 1536.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 7 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core.

The server includes 11400 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 75 Gbps. It supports advanced CPU instruction sets, including Intel Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for deep learning and matrix multiplication workloads. Cryptographic acceleration is supported via AES, VAES, and SHA_NI instruction sets.

In benchmark testing, the r8id.metal-48xl demonstrates top-tier multi-core performance, scoring in the top 10% for stress-ng multi-core CPU tests, PassMark CPU, and Geekbench multi-core workloads. Its memory subsystem achieves top-tier read and write bandwidth for large blocks. Database operations and Redis SET operations also rank in the top 10%, processing 240.29 million operations per second. For AI workloads, the server delivers strong to top-tier performance in LLM inference prompt processing and text generation across small, medium, and large models.

Given its bare-metal architecture, high memory density, and substantial local NVMe storage, this server offers high resource density for enterprise workloads. It is highly suited for large-scale databases, in-memory caching layers, high-throughput web serving, software compilation, and medium-to-large LLM inference tasks where GPU acceleration is not required.
description: Amazon Web Services r8id.metal-48xl is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server featuring 192 dedicated vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 6975P-C processors running at 3.9 GHz. The system is equipped with 1536.0 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory and 11400 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. It delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 75 Gbps and supports advanced instruction sets like Intel AMX and AVX-512. In benchmark evaluations, the server achieves top-tier multi-core CPU performance, exceptional memory bandwidth, and outstanding database throughput, processing 240.29 million Redis SET operations per second. It also exhibits strong performance in LLM inference tasks for medium and large models. This hardware profile is highly suited for memory-intensive databases, large-scale software compilation, in-memory caching, and data-intensive enterprise workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r8id.metal-48xl is a bare-metal server with 192 vCPUs, 1536 GB DDR5 RAM, and 11.4 TB NVMe SSD, delivering top-tier multi-core and database performance for enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r8id.metal-48xl is a bare-metal server with 192 vCPUs, 1536 GB DDR5 RAM, and 11400 GB NVMe SSD for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A high-density bare-metal platform combining massive DDR5 memory, local NVMe storage, and top-tier multi-core performance for demanding enterprise databases.
bullet_points: 192 dedicated vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 6975P-C processors at 3.9 GHz.
1536.0 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory and 11400 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.
Top-tier multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and database benchmark performance.
75 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for high-throughput data transfer.
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated matrix and vector operations.
Optimized for large-scale databases, in-memory caching, and LLM inference.
